rules.instruction: Separate production implementation, CLI adaptation, and test adaptation into distinct micro-tasks when possible.
A task should introduce or change one behavior only.
Build a helper first, integrate it second, expose the result third, and change policy last.
Provide only the files needed to complete the current task.
Every task must define files available to read and files allowed to modify.
Commit only after tests, diff checks, guard rails, and review all pass.
A task that changes a public function signature must include or be followed immediately by tasks updating every caller.
Introduce a new enum, dataclass, or interface in one task before integrating it into control flow.
When a new warning appears, create a focused cleanup task instead of accepting warning growth.
Choose domain names that do not conflict with framework conventions, such as pytest test-class discovery.
